title: dianthus fruticosus subsp. carpathus
intro: Dianthus fruticosus subsp. carpathus, also known as the alpine pink or fringed pink, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Dianthus frutcosus subsp. carpathus include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
